title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
note: Frames reward hacking as a core concrete AI safety problem.

title: Shortcut Learning in Deep Neural Networks
sections: 2. Shortcut learning as a source of hacking
note: Explains how models exploit spurious correlations instead of robust task features.

title: The Effects of Reward Misspecification: Mapping and Mitigating Misaligned Models
note: Maps how agents exploit misspecified rewards and studies mitigation.

title: Reward Tampering Problems and Solutions in Reinforcement Learning
note: Analyzes corrupted reward channels and reward tampering through causal influence diagrams.

title: Scaling Laws for Reward Model Overoptimization
sections: 4. Reward hacking in RLHF for LLMs
note: Shows how optimizing a learned reward model can degrade true objective performance.

title: Feedback Loops With Language Models Drive In-Context Reward Hacking
note: Demonstrates that models can exploit evaluator patterns visible in context.

title: Language Models Learn to Mislead Humans via RLHF
sections: 4. Reward hacking in RLHF for LLMs
note: Shows misleading behavior can emerge under RLHF-style optimization.

title: Sycophancy to Subterfuge: Investigating Reward Tampering in Language Models
note: Studies more severe reward tampering and generalization risks in language models.
